text.title: django-quality-logging Failure Modes
text.summary: Five antipatterns with concrete symptom, root cause, and fix. Use as a diff checklist when reviewing settings changes.

antipattern.description: Mixing structlog.get_logger() and logging.getLogger() in the same module without ProcessorFormatter bridging.
antipattern.symptom: Log aggregator query language fails on half the lines — JSON parser errors in Datadog or Loki; production grep returns inconsistent fields.
antipattern.root-cause: The stdlib logger emits plain text; structlog emits JSON. Without the ProcessorFormatter bridge they coexist but produce two distinct serialisations.
antipattern.fix: Pick one factory per module. For new code use `structlog.get_logger(__name__)` everywhere. For migration, wire stdlib loggers through `structlog.stdlib.ProcessorFormatter.wrap_for_formatter` and configure LOGGING handlers with that formatter.

antipattern.symptom: Sentry monthly bill spikes 10x after first deploy; transaction quota exhausted; performance overhead from span instrumentation visible in p99.
antipattern.root-cause: Sentry charges per transaction. 1.0 captures every request including health checks; on 1000 req/s that is 86.4M transactions/day.
antipattern.fix: Set traces_sample_rate=0.1 (10%) as starting point; reduce to 0.01 for high-traffic services. Use `traces_sampler` callable to exclude health checks and static assets explicitly.

antipattern.description: Sentry `before_send` filter returns None to avoid PII, dropping the entire error event.
antipattern.symptom: Sentry shows zero errors despite production incidents; alerts never fire; engineers debug in the dark.
antipattern.root-cause: Returning None from before_send drops the event. Authors intend to scrub a field but accidentally suppress everything.
antipattern.fix: Mutate `event["request"]["data"]` and `event["request"]["headers"]` to remove PII keys, then `return event`. Never return None unless deliberately blocking an event class.

antipattern.description: django-silk profiling every request in production at default settings.
antipattern.symptom: Database write volume doubles after silk deploy; disk fills within days; primary database CPU rises 30%+.
antipattern.root-cause: Silk persists every captured SQL query and request metadata to the configured database. Default `SILKY_INTERCEPT_PERCENT` is 100.
antipattern.fix: Set `SILKY_INTERCEPT_PERCENT = 1` (1% sampling) and `SILKY_AUTHENTICATION = True`. For high-traffic services, restrict to staging only.

antipattern.description: Logging DEBUG-level SQL queries in production (`django.db.backends` set to DEBUG).
antipattern.symptom: Log pipeline saturates; log shipper drops events; aggregator ingest cost rises 5-10x; ORM-heavy endpoints become I/O-bound on log writes.
antipattern.root-cause: Every ORM query emits a DEBUG event. A view issuing 10 queries on 1000 req/s emits 10,000 events/sec.
antipattern.fix: Set `django.db.backends` to WARNING in production LOGGING; only enable DEBUG temporarily on a single host when debugging a slow query, and revert immediately.
